title: Backend Framework Migration
document:
[E001 | 2026-01-03T09:00:00 | Aria | decision] The team chose Flask as the backend framework for the CareerPilot app because everyone already knew it.
[E002 | 2026-01-04T11:30:00 | Kabir | decision] Deployment was planned on Railway, with Supabase used only for authentication.
[E003 | 2026-01-07T15:20:00 | Mira | requirement] The team found that async endpoints and OpenAPI docs would be important for the interview simulator.
[E004 | 2026-01-08T10:10:00 | Aria | update] The backend framework decision was changed from Flask to FastAPI to support async routes and automatic OpenAPI documentation.
[E005 | 2026-01-09T17:40:00 | Kabir | confirmation] Railway remained the deployment target after the framework change.
[E006 | 2026-01-11T13:00:00 | Mira | rejected_idea] The team decided not to use Django because the current API surface is small and the ORM is not needed.

title: Roadmap Feature Scope
document:
[E001 | 2026-02-01T10:00:00 | Nisha | requirement] The roadmap feature will initially support four target roles: AI Engineer, ML Engineer, Data Scientist, and Applied Scientist.
[E002 | 2026-02-02T14:00:00 | Dev | decision] The first plan was to generate roadmaps using only the uploaded resume.
[E003 | 2026-02-04T09:30:00 | Nisha | update] Market trend data was added as a required input because the roadmap should reflect current role demand.
[E004 | 2026-02-05T16:00:00 | Omar | rejected_idea] The team decided that LinkedIn profile scraping would not be included in v1 because of privacy and reliability risks.
[E005 | 2026-02-07T11:15:00 | Dev | requirement] The v1 roadmap output must include weekly milestones, skill gaps, project suggestions, and interview preparation tasks.
[E006 | 2026-02-08T12:45:00 | Nisha | update] The resume-only generation plan is outdated; the accepted design uses resume plus selected role plus market trends.

title: Payment Integration Plan
document:
[E001 | 2026-03-02T09:15:00 | Leena | decision] The team planned to integrate Razorpay before deployment using ngrok for local webhook testing.
[E002 | 2026-03-02T13:30:00 | Ravi | requirement] The webhook endpoint will be /api/billing/webhook and should listen for payment.captured events.
[E003 | 2026-03-03T10:00:00 | Leena | update] Because the site is not deployed yet, the team decided to postpone live Razorpay verification.
[E004 | 2026-03-03T11:45:00 | Ravi | update] The team decided to use test mode now and complete production payment testing after deployment.
[E005 | 2026-03-04T16:00:00 | Maya | update] The ngrok setup was dropped for the current sprint to avoid unnecessary complexity.
[E006 | 2026-03-05T12:10:00 | Leena | decision] The final sprint scope is to store Razorpay environment variables and leave full webhook testing for post-deployment.

title: Research Experiment Compute Constraints
document:
[E001 | 2026-04-01T08:30:00 | Ishan | decision] The first experiment plan was to fine-tune a 7B model for all memory extraction tasks.
[E002 | 2026-04-01T18:00:00 | Sara | constraint] The group confirmed that no paid APIs should be required for the benchmark prototype.
[E003 | 2026-04-02T09:20:00 | Ishan | update] Because local compute is CPU-only, the 7B fine-tuning plan is not feasible for v0.
[E004 | 2026-04-02T14:10:00 | Sara | decision] The new plan is to use prompt-based extraction with free-tier hosted LLMs and open-source embeddings.
[E005 | 2026-04-03T10:00:00 | Meera | requirement] For reproducibility, every baseline must use the same embedding model, chunk size, LLM, and question set.
[E006 | 2026-04-04T17:40:00 | Ishan | deferred_idea] Fine-tuning can be reconsidered only after the v0 benchmark and baseline results are complete.

title: Interview Simulator Voice Design
document:
[E001 | 2026-05-02T10:30:00 | Tara | decision] The initial web voice plan used the browser SpeechSynthesis API with whatever default voice the browser selected.
[E002 | 2026-05-02T14:00:00 | Kunal | fact] The platform includes male and female interviewer personas across companies.
[E003 | 2026-05-03T09:15:00 | Tara | update] The voice assignment logic was updated: male interviewer personas should use available male voices, and female personas should use available female voices.
[E004 | 2026-05-03T16:20:00 | Kunal | fallback] If the browser does not expose a matching gendered voice, the app should fall back to the clearest available English voice.
[E005 | 2026-05-04T11:00:00 | Tara | rejected_idea] Hosted TTS APIs are not included in v1 because the project should minimize recurring costs.
[E006 | 2026-05-05T15:25:00 | Kunal | requirement] Users should be able to replay the spoken question, but voice cloning is explicitly out of scope.

title: Question Bank Company Tags
document:
[E001 | 2026-06-01T09:00:00 | Priya | decision] The first tagging plan tried to balance every company tag equally across all question banks.
[E002 | 2026-06-01T13:15:00 | Arjun | fact] The company field is stored as an array, not as a plain text string.
[E003 | 2026-06-02T10:30:00 | Priya | update] The tagging rule changed: company tags should be selected based on realistic topic-company fit, not forced balancing.
[E004 | 2026-06-02T11:45:00 | Arjun | constraint] Popular companies like MAANG, Microsoft, and OpenAI should still appear where relevant, but not in every bank.
[E005 | 2026-06-03T16:30:00 | Priya | requirement] For SQL queries against the company field, array-aware operators must be used instead of equality against a string.
[E006 | 2026-06-04T08:50:00 | Arjun | rejected_idea] The team rejected blind company-count balancing because it produces unrealistic interview tags.

title: Cloud Deployment Strategy
document:
[E001 | 2026-07-02T09:20:00 | Sameer | decision] The first deployment idea used a Python backend on a traditional VM with manual process management.
[E002 | 2026-07-02T14:50:00 | Lina | fact] The app depends mainly on Supabase for storage and auth, and external model APIs for generation.
[E003 | 2026-07-03T10:10:00 | Sameer | update] Cloudflare became the preferred deployment target because the app is mostly frontend/API orchestration and needs low cost.
[E004 | 2026-07-03T18:25:00 | Lina | constraint] The team decided not to host large models directly in the first 2-3 months.
[E005 | 2026-07-04T12:15:00 | Sameer | conditional_decision] If Python-only services are required later, they will be isolated behind separate serverless or container endpoints.
[E006 | 2026-07-05T16:00:00 | Lina | update] The VM deployment plan is no longer preferred because it increases maintenance effort before product validation.

title: Paper Experiment Scope
document:
[E001 | 2026-08-01T09:00:00 | Yash | decision] The first paper plan evaluated only one synthetic document and one RAG baseline.
[E002 | 2026-08-01T17:00:00 | Rhea | constraint] The target venue requires strong baselines and controlled comparisons.
[E003 | 2026-08-02T10:40:00 | Yash | update] The experiment plan was expanded to compare vanilla RAG, rerank RAG, contextual retrieval, agentic RAG, GraphRAG, and living memory.
[E004 | 2026-08-02T14:00:00 | Rhea | requirement] Every system must answer with final answer, evidence IDs, retrieved context IDs, latency, and token count.
[E005 | 2026-08-03T11:30:00 | Yash | rejected_idea] The one-baseline plan was rejected because it would not support a strong publication claim.
[E006 | 2026-08-04T15:45:00 | Rhea | requirement] Ablations must include no temporal validity, no contradiction handling, and no provenance reranking.

title: Study Plan Change
document:
[E001 | 2026-09-01T08:00:00 | User | plan] I will revise probability first, then move to linear algebra next week.
[E002 | 2026-09-02T20:00:00 | User | update] The ML interview is now scheduled earlier than expected, so I need to prioritize ML fundamentals.
[E003 | 2026-09-03T09:30:00 | Assistant | decision] The updated study order is ML fundamentals first, then probability, then linear algebra.
[E004 | 2026-09-04T18:00:00 | User | constraint] I only have 90 minutes per day on weekdays.
[E005 | 2026-09-05T07:45:00 | User | rejected_idea] Skip deep reinforcement learning for now because it is unlikely for this interview.
[E006 | 2026-09-06T21:00:00 | Assistant | update] The earlier probability-first plan is superseded by the ML-fundamentals-first plan.

title: Data Pipeline Ownership
document:
[E001 | 2026-10-01T10:00:00 | Neel | decision] The ETL pipeline was initially assigned to the backend team.
[E002 | 2026-10-01T15:20:00 | Asha | fact] The pipeline has three stages: extract from Kaggle, transform column names, and load into Supabase.
[E003 | 2026-10-02T11:00:00 | Neel | update] Ownership changed because the data team needs to validate schema and quality before loading.
[E004 | 2026-10-02T11:05:00 | Asha | decision] The data team is now the DRI for ETL, while backend owns only the API that reads from Supabase.
[E005 | 2026-10-03T14:40:00 | Neel | constraint] The backend team should not modify transform logic without data team review.
[E006 | 2026-10-04T09:10:00 | Asha | update] The old backend-owned ETL assignment is no longer valid.

title: Model Monitoring Policy
document:
[E001 | 2026-11-01T09:00:00 | Farah | decision] The first monitoring plan checked only model accuracy once per month.
[E002 | 2026-11-01T12:00:00 | Leo | requirement] The team wants to detect feature drift before accuracy drops significantly.
[E003 | 2026-11-02T10:00:00 | Farah | update] The monitoring plan was updated to compute PSI weekly for key numerical and categorical features.
[E004 | 2026-11-02T16:00:00 | Leo | decision] Retraining should be triggered if PSI crosses the alert threshold for two consecutive checks.
[E005 | 2026-11-03T11:00:00 | Farah | update] The monthly-accuracy-only monitoring plan is obsolete.
[E006 | 2026-11-04T17:15:00 | Leo | requirement] The v0 dashboard should show drift status, last check time, and whether retraining is recommended.

title: Content Moderation Decision Rules
document:
[E001 | 2026-12-01T10:10:00 | Hema | decision] The first moderation rule blocked every negative comment.
[E002 | 2026-12-01T13:25:00 | Rohan | fact] The classifier outputs toxicity, sentiment, and spam labels.
[E003 | 2026-12-02T09:45:00 | Hema | update] The moderation rule was revised: toxic or spam comments should be blocked, but negative non-toxic comments should be allowed with warning.
[E004 | 2026-12-02T15:00:00 | Rohan | requirement] Neutral comments should be allowed unless they are toxic or spam.
[E005 | 2026-12-03T11:30:00 | Hema | rejected_idea] The block-all-negative rule was rejected because it suppresses valid criticism.
[E006 | 2026-12-04T16:20:00 | Rohan | requirement] The UI must display the moderation decision as allow, warn, or block.

title: Architecture Review Responsibility
document:
[E001 | 2027-01-05T09:00:00 | Milan | decision] The initial architecture review process required only the feature team's engineering manager approval.
[E002 | 2027-01-05T14:20:00 | Reva | risk] The new caching layer may affect authentication, billing, and analytics workflows.
[E003 | 2027-01-06T10:15:00 | Milan | update] Because the change is far-reaching, review now requires the feature EM, security representative, and platform owner.
[E004 | 2027-01-06T16:00:00 | Reva | requirement] A rollout plan with monitoring metrics is required before release.
[E005 | 2027-01-07T11:30:00 | Milan | update] The EM-only approval process is no longer sufficient for this caching change.
[E006 | 2027-01-08T13:45:00 | Reva | decision] The team decided not to release the caching layer behind a permanent dark launch; feature flags will be temporary.

title: Customer Escalation Workflow
document:
[E001 | 2027-02-01T09:15:00 | Anika | decision] The first escalation workflow routed all customer bugs through the normal backlog.
[E002 | 2027-02-01T12:30:00 | Jules | incident] A critical enterprise customer reported that the bug blocks their month-end reporting process.
[E003 | 2027-02-02T10:00:00 | Anika | update] The workflow changed: critical business-impact bugs get priority::1 and must be scheduled within one business day after required approvals.
[E004 | 2027-02-02T15:40:00 | Jules | requirement] Daily progress updates must be posted in the escalated customer Slack channel.
[E005 | 2027-02-03T11:20:00 | Anika | update] The normal-backlog-only route is no longer valid for approved critical escalations.
[E006 | 2027-02-04T16:10:00 | Jules | constraint] Feature requests still require Product DRI approval even if they come from the escalated account.

title: Meeting Notes Memory
document:
[E001 | 2027-03-01T10:00:00 | User | task] For the demo, prepare a Streamlit dashboard with three charts.
[E002 | 2027-03-01T11:30:00 | User | update] The demo audience changed from internal teammates to external investors.
[E003 | 2027-03-01T14:00:00 | User | update] Because investors care about traction, replace one technical chart with a user growth chart.
[E004 | 2027-03-02T09:00:00 | Assistant | decision] The current demo dashboard should include revenue trend, user growth, and model reliability charts.
[E005 | 2027-03-02T13:00:00 | User | constraint] Do not include raw database latency unless someone asks during Q&A.
[E006 | 2027-03-03T16:00:00 | Assistant | update] The original three-technical-charts plan is superseded by the investor-focused chart plan.

title: Security Review Scope
document:
[E001 | 2027-04-05T09:00:00 | Vikram | decision] The initial security review covered only authentication endpoints.
[E002 | 2027-04-05T15:00:00 | Nora | risk] The new billing feature touches authentication, invoices, payment webhooks, and user entitlements.
[E003 | 2027-04-06T10:30:00 | Vikram | update] Security review scope was expanded to include payment webhooks and entitlement changes.
[E004 | 2027-04-06T14:20:00 | Nora | constraint] Invoice display changes require privacy review but not a full security threat model.
[E005 | 2027-04-07T11:10:00 | Vikram | update] The auth-only review scope is obsolete for the billing release.
[E006 | 2027-04-08T13:50:00 | Nora | requirement] The release cannot ship until webhook signature verification is tested.

title: Research Dataset Plan
document:
[E001 | 2027-05-01T09:30:00 | Amit | decision] The first benchmark plan used only real company handbooks.
[E002 | 2027-05-01T17:00:00 | Zoya | constraint] Manual annotation of long real documents is too slow for the current deadline.
[E003 | 2027-05-02T10:00:00 | Amit | update] The dataset plan changed to synthetic scenario streams with gold memory atoms and evidence IDs.
[E004 | 2027-05-02T12:20:00 | Zoya | deferred_idea] Real documents may still be used later as an external validation set.
[E005 | 2027-05-03T11:45:00 | Amit | requirement] Every synthetic scenario must include at least one update, one superseded fact, and one abstention question.
[E006 | 2027-05-04T15:30:00 | Zoya | update] The real-handbook-only plan is dropped for v0 because it creates too much manual work.

title: Agent Tool Permission Policy
document:
[E001 | 2027-06-01T08:45:00 | Eli | decision] The initial agent policy allowed the coding agent to run shell commands automatically after planning.
[E002 | 2027-06-01T13:20:00 | Mina | risk] Some shell commands can modify files or delete generated artifacts.
[E003 | 2027-06-02T09:50:00 | Eli | update] The policy changed: read-only commands may run automatically, but write/delete commands require user approval.
[E004 | 2027-06-02T14:10:00 | Mina | constraint] Network calls are disabled unless the task explicitly requires external data.
[E005 | 2027-06-03T11:00:00 | Eli | update] The unrestricted automatic shell command policy is no longer valid.
[E006 | 2027-06-04T15:30:00 | Mina | requirement] The agent must log each tool call with timestamp, command category, and approval status.
